title: Dias, Meses e Alimentação
subtitle: Days, Months, Food & Time Prepositions
description: Learn days of the week, months, birthday dates, food vocabulary, mealtimes, and time prepositions.

vocabulary.pronunciation: seh-GOON-dah FAY-rah, tehr-SAH FAY-rah, KWAHR-tah FAY-rah
vocabulary.tip: In Portugal, days use 'feira' after the number: segunda (2nd), terça (3rd), etc.

vocabulary.pronunciation: KEEN-tah FAY-rah, SAYSH-tah FAY-rah, SAH-bah-doo, doo-MEEN-goo
vocabulary.example: Sábado e domingo é o fim de semana.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Saturday and Sunday is the weekend.
vocabulary.tip: Sábado and domingo don't use 'feira'. Weekend = fim de semana

vocabulary.example: O meu aniversário é em janeiro.
vocabulary.tip: Months are NOT capitalized in Portuguese (unlike English)

vocabulary.example: — Quando fazes anos? — Faço anos no dia 3 de agosto.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: — When is your birthday? — My birthday is on August 3rd.
vocabulary.tip: 'Fazer anos' = to have a birthday (literally: to make years)

vocabulary.exampleTranslation: — How old are you turning? — I'm turning 21.
vocabulary.tip: Literally: 'How many years do you make?' — same as 'Quantos anos tens?'

vocabulary.exampleTranslation: In the morning, we have breakfast.
vocabulary.tip: Verb: 'tomar o pequeno-almoço' (to have breakfast). In Brazil: 'café da manhã'

vocabulary.example: Entre as 12 e as 14 horas, almoçamos.
vocabulary.tip: Verb: 'almoçar' (to have lunch). Lunch is the main meal in Portugal, usually 12-2pm

vocabulary.exampleTranslation: In the afternoon, we have a snack.
vocabulary.tip: Verb: 'lanchar' (to have a snack). The Portuguese afternoon snack, usually around 4-5pm

vocabulary.exampleTranslation: In the evening, we have dinner.
vocabulary.tip: Verb: 'jantar' (to have dinner). Dinner in Portugal is usually later, around 8-9pm

vocabulary.example: Em janeiro. De manhã. À segunda-feira.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: In January. In the morning. On Monday.
vocabulary.tip: 'Em' for months/years, 'de' for parts of day, 'a' for specific days/hours

vocabulary.word: às duas da tarde / à noite / ao meio-dia
vocabulary.pronunciation: ahsh DOO-ash dah TAR-deh / ah NOY-teh / oh MEH-yoo DEE-ah
vocabulary.ipa: /aʃ ˈduɐʒ dɐ ˈtaɾdɨ/ / /ɐ ˈnojtɨ/ / /aw ˈmeju ˈdiɐ/
vocabulary.example: Tenho aula às duas da tarde.
vocabulary.tip: 'À' = a + a (at the). 'Ao' = a + o (at the). Always use the contraction!

sections.heading: Days of the Week
sections.body: In Portuguese, the days of the week are based on a numbering system. Sunday is 'domingo' (Lord's day), and the rest are numbered: segunda-feira (2nd fair = Monday), terça-feira (3rd fair = Tuesday), quarta-feira (4th fair = Wednesday), quinta-feira (5th fair = Thursday), sexta-feira (6th fair = Friday). Saturday is 'sábado' (from Sabbath). In Portugal, when referring to a specific day, use the preposition 'a' which contracts with the article: 'à segunda-feira' (on Monday). For repeated/habitual actions on a day, also use 'à': 'À segunda-feira tenho aulas' (On Mondays I have classes).

sections.heading: Months of the Year
sections.body: Portuguese months are very similar to English and come from the same Latin roots. They are NOT capitalized in Portuguese. When saying something happens in a particular month, use the preposition 'em': 'em janeiro' (in January), 'em março' (in March). Unlike days, months don't contract with articles in this context.

sections.heading: Talking About Birthdays — Fazer Anos
sections.body: In Portuguese, you don't say 'My birthday is...' — you say 'I make years': 'Faço anos'. The verb 'fazer' (to make/do) is used with 'anos' (years) to talk about birthdays. To ask someone's birthday: 'Quando fazes anos?' (When do you have your birthday?). To ask how old someone is turning: 'Quantos anos fazes?' (How many years do you make?). To give a date: 'Faço anos no dia 3 de agosto' (My birthday is on August 3rd). Note the structure: 'no dia + number + de + month'.

sections.heading: Fazer — Present Tense (for talking about birthdays)
Time Prepositions: EM, DE, A
sections.body: Portuguese uses three main prepositions to locate actions in time: EM (in), DE (of/from), and A (at). Each contracts with definite articles: em + o = no, em + a = na, de + o = do, de + a = da, a + o = ao, a + a = às. The general rules: EM for months, years, and general time periods ('em janeiro', 'em 2026', 'em Portugal'). DE for parts of the day and origins ('de manhã', 'de tarde', 'de noite'). A for specific days and clock times ('à segunda-feira', 'às duas da tarde').

sections.heading: Time Prepositions — When to Use Each
rows: de manhã, de tarde, de noite
à segunda-feira, às duas da tarde
no dia 14 de junho, no sábado

sections.heading: Meals & Eating Habits in Portugal
sections.body: Portuguese eating culture has four main meals: pequeno-almoço (breakfast, morning), almoço (lunch, 12-2pm — the main meal), lanche (afternoon snack, 4-5pm), and jantar (dinner, 8-9pm). Each meal has its own verb: 'tomar o pequeno-almoço' (to have breakfast), 'almoçar' (to have lunch), 'lanchar' (to snack), 'jantar' (to have dinner). Portuguese meal times are typically later than in the US or UK, especially dinner.

items.portuguese: À segunda-feira tenho aulas de manhã e de tarde.
items.translation: On Monday I have classes in the morning and afternoon.
items.pronunciation: ah seh-GOON-dah FAY-rah TEN-yoo ah-oo-lash deh mah-NYAH ee deh TAR-deh.

items.portuguese: Faço anos no dia 14 de maio.
items.pronunciation: FAH-soo AH-noosh noh DEE-ah DEH-zee deh MY-yoo.

items.portuguese: Almoçamos ao meio-dia e jantamos às nove da noite.
items.translation: We have lunch at noon and dinner at 9pm.
items.pronunciation: ahl-moh-SAH-moosh oh MEH-yoo DEE-ee-ah ee zhan-TAH-moosh ahsh NOH-veh dah NOY-teh.

sections.heading: Common Mistakes & Tips
items: Days of the week are NOT capitalized in Portuguese: 'segunda-feira' (not 'Segunda-feira').
Use 'à' (not 'em') before days of the week: 'à segunda-feira' (on Monday), NOT 'em segunda-feira'.
Use 'de' for parts of the day: 'de manhã' (in the morning), 'de tarde' (in the afternoon), 'de noite' (at night).
Use 'a' for clock times: 'às duas' (at 2 o'clock), 'à meia-noite' (at midnight).
When giving a date, use 'no dia + number + de + month': 'no dia 24 de abril'.
The verb for 'to have breakfast' is 'tomar o pequeno-almoço' — literally 'to take breakfast'.

exercises.title: Choose the Correct Time Preposition
exercises.instructions: Select the correct preposition for each time expression.

items.question: ___ janeiro faz frio.
items.explanation: Use 'em' for months: 'Em janeiro' = In January.

items.question: ___ segunda-feira tenho aulas.
items.explanation: Use 'a' for days of the week: 'à segunda-feira' = on Monday.

items.question: ___ manhã bebo café.
items.explanation: Use 'de' for parts of the day: 'de manhã' = in the morning.

items.question: ___ duas da tarde tenho aula.
items.explanation: Use 'a' for clock times: 'às duas da tarde' = at 2pm.

exercises.title: Write the Date in Portuguese
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank with the correct date expression.

items.sentence: My birthday is on March 15th. → Faço anos ___ 15 ___ março.

exercises.title: Match the Meal
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct meal for each description.

items.question: Which meal is between 12 and 2pm?
items.explanation: 'O almoço' is lunch, the main midday meal in Portugal (12-2pm).

items.question: Which meal is in the morning?
items.explanation: 'O pequeno-almoço' is breakfast, eaten in the morning.

items.question: Which meal is at 8-9pm?
items.explanation: 'O jantar' is dinner, eaten in the evening (8-9pm in Portugal).

exercises.title: Build Sentences About Your Routine
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct time expression to complete each sentence.

items.hint: Use 'a' for days of the week
Use 'de' for parts of the day
